title: Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion
content:
{{Top|canon=Neimoidian Royal Guard}} {{Otheruses|Gunnery|more=Royal Guard}} {{Organization |image=[[File:Neimoidian_Guard_EP3.png]] |name=Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion |type= |founder= |leader= |subgroup= |headquarters= |locations= |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] |restored= |affiliation=*[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] }} {{Quote|'What happened?'''"
"''Some kind of ''weapons malfunction'', from the looks of it, sir!|[[Unidentified Neimoidian councilor|A Neimoidian councilor]] and a Neimoidian guard|{{TCWComicCite|6.53|Update}}}} The '''Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion''', also known as the '''Royal Guard''', was a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] military unit created by the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]] of the Confederacy of Independent Systems to provide important officials with sentient guards when it was too risky to use [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that were vulnerable to centralized deactivation. ==Description== [[File:NeimoidianSniper-ROTSGG.png|thumb|left|150px|A Neimoidian sniper]] The Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion,{{InsiderCite|147|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: The Devious Neimoidians Revealed}} which in fact was composed of multiple [[battalion]]s identified by the same name,{{InsiderCite|76|CIS Shadowfeed Dispatch 15:3:21 Edition}} was a military force comprised of [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] who wore distinctive bronze-colored [[Plastoid/Legends|plastoid]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]] that mimicked an ancient [[beetle-knight]] design from a pre-industrial era. Most members carry ceremonial blaster rifles which loosely resemble the shape of ancient black powder-based weaponry. The [[Sniper/Legends|snipers]] wore the same armor as that of the warriors, but the uniform was a bottle-[[Color/Legends|green]]. [[Commander/Legends|Commanders]]' armor and battle staves were silver and bore the symbol of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] (CIS) after its formation, with the staves being mass-produced for commanders during the Clone Wars.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (toy line)|pack=#63: Neimoidian Commander (Separatist Bodyguard)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/ROTS0563neimoidian.asp}} They were also wielded [[Weequay blaster pistol/Legends|Weequay blaster pistols]] on board [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]. Aboard [[Sib Canay/Legends|Sib Canay's]] [[Unidentified Lucrehulk-class battleship (Pantora)/Legends|Lucrehulk battleship]] the guards wore silver-colored armor that differed from standard guard armor.{{TCW|Sphere of Influence}} Most members of the battalion were recruited from the lower levels of Neimoidian society, primarily criminals and mercenaries, who were offered amnesty or commuted sentences in exchange for protecting the Neimoidian delegation from would-be assassins.[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'': Prima Official Game Guide]] Others were raised from [[Grub-hatchery|grubhood]] to be above bribery, making them strangely noble and selfless for Neimoidians.''[[Star Wars Chronicles: The Prequels]]'' [[File:NeimoidianBrute-ROTSGG.png|thumb|right|180px|A Neimoidian brute]] Neimoidian brute guards were selected from among the most volatile and aggressive members of Neimoidian security forces and enrolled in a specialized brute program, often against their will. As part of this program, candidates were subjected to extreme [[Bioengineering/Legends|genetic enhancements]] that significantly increased their physical size and strength. The resulting guards were highly unstable combatants, prone to violent outbursts and berserker-like behavior, including attacks against fellow guards. Due to their unpredictability, Neimoidian brute guards were frequently kept in stasis and deployed only in critical situations. In combat, Neimoidian brute guards had great durability and ferocity. Armed with heavy [[Vibro-weapon/Legends|vibro maces]], they were capable of deflecting [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] strikes, rendering conventional engagements ineffective. [[Neimoidian Sniper/Legends|Neimoidian snipers]] were specialists trained in long-range combat tactics and precision marksmanship and earned a reputation as some of the Confederacy of Independent Systems’ most accurate and lethal shooters. They operated in coordination with Neimoidian guard units and positioned themselves in concealed vantage points, where they remained motionless for extended periods, sometimes lasting several days, while awaiting their targets. Snipers were equipped with long rifles optimized for distance and accuracy and were deployed to elevated or otherwise inaccessible positions to preserve their tactical advantage. Although they were vulnerable at close range, their placement and discipline made direct engagement difficult. ==History== ===Organic forces=== {{Quote|The ion storms of Moorja have prevented the deployment of our standard battle-droid forces, but Neimoidian Gunnery Battalions, Gossam Commandos and Koorivar Fusiliers have consolidated to provide Moorja with cost-effective protective forces.|Commerce Guild Presidente Shu Mai, regarding the deployment of the Gunnery Battalion to Moorja|}} [[File:CIS_Troops.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Neimoidian Gunnery Battalions were amongst the ranks of the Moorja defense force.]] During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion was deployed to the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid-Rim]] planet [[Moorja/Legends|Moorja]], located down the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] from [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]]. According to [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] President [[Shu Mai/Legends|Shu Mai]], [[Ion storm/Legends|ion storms]] on Moorja had prevented the use of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|standard battle droids]]. As a result, Neimoidian Gunnery Battalions, along with other organic forces, were consolidated to provide protection for the planet. Shu Mai described this approach as more affordable than refitting droid forces with ionic-damping technologies. She also noted that Moorja’s local agrarian yields could be used to feed the deployed forces. The use of organic troops, she explained, was part of a broader strategy to protect a vulnerable back door to the recently conquered section of the Corellian Trade Spine. Around [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],{{TCWdates|304}} several members of the Gunnery Battalion were stationed on board the Trade Federation's [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] during the [[Blockade of Pantora/Legends|blockade of Pantora]]. A [[Riyo Chuchi/Legends|Riyo Chuchi]] and a disguised [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] Land inside the [[Sib Canay/Legends|Sib Canay's]] [[Unidentified Lucrehulk-class battleship (Pantora)/Legends|Lucrehulk battleship]] two Neimoidians Guarded the Hangar door. While sneaking through the ship, both women encountered a Neimoidian Guard monitoring a computer terminal. To avoid detection, Ahsoka used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] on a nearby container to distract the guard, allowing both of them to slip past unnoticed. A [[Unidentified Neimoidian guard/Legends|Neimoidian Guard]] was stationed outside the [[Detention area|detention block]] of [[Sib Canay/Legends|Sib Caney]]'s ship, informing others that the area was closed to visitors and ordering them to move along. [[File:AhsokaMindTrickFederationGuard-SphereofInfluence.png|thumb|right|300px|A Neimoidian guard was deceived by the Jedi Ahsoka Tano using a mind trick during the Pantora crisis.]] When Tano approached, he initially denied entry, but, after she used a [[Mind trick/Legends|Jedi mind trick]] and told him to let her pass, he complied. As Ahsoka proceeded, the guard returned to position, blocking Chuchi from following. This prompted Chuchi to call out to Ahsoka, who then repeated the mind trick, instructing the guard to let them both pass. The guard hesitated briefly, then obeyed and allowed them through. When captain [[Sib Canay/Legends|Sib Canay]] was pinned at the wall by [[Ahsoka Tano's lightsabers/Legends|Ahsoka Tano's Lightsaber]], the guards stormed in the [[Detention area|detention block]] followed by the Federation's [[Unidentified Neimoidian envoy/Legends|envoy]]. ===End of the war=== Around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],The events of "[[Update]]" are occur after "[[Bounty (episode)|Bounty]]"—which itself is set in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] according to [[The Official Star Wars Fact File Part 100|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' Part 100]] {{C|20 BBY 39-40: Battle of Dathomir}}—as "[[Mandalorian Memories]]" serves as a prequel to the episode. Considering that the [[Star Wars comic books|comics]] featured in [[Star Wars Comic|''Star Wars Comic UK'']] are published in chronological order, it can be concluded that "Update" takes place around 20 BBY. shortly after a [[Raid on a Separatist factory|raid on a separatist factory]], several Neimoidian soldiers at an [[Unidentified Separatist asteroid base|Seperatist Base]] escorted a B1 battle droid tasked with delivering plans for a new battle droid model. When the B1 overheard that the new design was intended to replace its series, it went rogue and fired on the control panel holding the plans, destroying them. Suspecting sabotage, [[Dooku/Legends|Count Dooku]] ordered the Neimoidian soldiers to inspect the it’s [[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] for signs of a weapons malfunction but they found nothing wrong. Believing the droid had acted on its own, Dooku commanded the soldiers to destroy it. The two Neimoidians complied, opening fire and terminating the B1. [[File:Separatist_prisoners_EGTW.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Two members of the battalion were taken as prisoners by Imperial personnel for suspected Separatist loyalities.]] In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' four Neimoidian guards were stationed in the antechamber outside Nute Gunray's suite to protect him and [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]] from [[Grievous/Legends|General Grievous]]. When Grievous arrived accompanied by his elite [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|MagnaGuards]], the droids raised their [[Electrostaff/Legends|electrostaffs]] and, with swift and precise strikes, swept the Neimoidian guards off their feet, effectively neutralizing them.''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' The Neimoidian [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] of the Separatist Council were also part of the Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion, being selected from seasoned officers such as [[Captain]] [[Gap Nox]],{{SW|url=news/the-trade-federation-and-neimoidians-a-history|text=The Trade Federation and Neimoidians: A History|nolive=1}} who had become one of Gunray's bodyguards after the [[Battle of Moorja (Clone Wars)|Battle of Moorja]], and [[Lieutenant]] [[Fame Drimal]], who had previously been stationed near the Separatist-aligned''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' planet{{WEGCite|story=Treasure Hunt (adventure)|book=Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)}} [[Plagen]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} The unit accompanied the Separatist leadership to [[Mustafar]], where two Neimoidian guards stood watch on the landing platform, overseeing a pair of Neimoidian aides who greeted by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious's]] newly appointed [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] upon his arrival. After Vader executed the aides, the guards attempted to engage him, but one was slain by his [[Darth Vader's lightsaber|lightsaber]] and the other killed by [[Force choke]]. The remaining bodyguards within the facility were subsequently [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|slaughtered]] by Vader along with the rest of the Separatist leadership. At one point during the ensuing [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], a duo of Neimoidian guards who were suspected of holding Separatist loyalties were cornered and held at blaster-point by a grouping of Imperial personnel, including orange-marked clone troopers. ==Behind the scenes== The Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion first appeared unamed in the [[2005]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars:'' Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. They were Identified as the Royal Guard in 2005's [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary|Revenge of the Sith Visual Dictionary]].''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]'' ===Concept art gallery=== {{Gallery |source=''[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith]]'' |storyboards= |concept= File:Neimoidian_Gunnery_Battalion_Abyssin_Concept.jpg|Abyssin
